channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: House to vote on Trump's war powers. And, Minnesota sues over halted Medicaid funding
item.description: The House is set to vote today on whether to constrain President Trump's authority to continue to wage war on Iran. And, Minnesota sues the Trump administration over halted Medicaid funding.
item.content:encoded:
The House is set to vote today on whether to constrain President Trump's authority to continue to wage war on Iran. And, Minnesota sues the Trump administration over halted Medicaid funding.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: U.S.-Israeli strikes continue across Iran; Iranian drones hit Azerbaijan
item.description: The U.S. and Israel say they conducted new strikes inside Iran overnight, targeting ballistic missile launchers. Iran claims it struck a U.S. oil tanker in the northern Persian Gulf.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Israel say they conducted new strikes inside Iran overnight, targeting ballistic missile launchers. Iran claims it struck a U.S. oil tanker in the northern Persian Gulf.

(Image credit: Ali Ihsan Ozturk)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: In lawsuit, Minnesota accuses Trump administration of 'weaponizing' Medicaid funding
item.description: The federal government said the state should do more to fight fraud and is holding back funds. Minnesota officials say the attack is unfair as the state's fraud rate is well below national averages.
item.content:encoded:
The federal government said the state should do more to fight fraud and is holding back funds. Minnesota officials say the attack is unfair as the state's fraud rate is well below national averages.

(Image credit: Tom Williams)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Wall Street is betting on tariff refunds after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: When the Supreme Court struck down many of President Trump's tariffs, it left importers wondering how long they'd have to wait to get their money back. Hedge funds are offering to help out.
item.content:encoded:
When the Supreme Court struck down many of President Trump's tariffs, it left importers wondering how long they'd have to wait to get their money back. Hedge funds are offering to help out.

(Image credit: Spencer Platt)
item.dc:creator: Vito Emanuel

item.title: March (Audio) Madness! Here are the finalists in NPR's College Podcast Challenge
item.description: From 75 campuses across 35 states, we've listened to hundreds of student entries to select the very best for NPR's College Podcast Challenge.
item.content:encoded: From 75 campuses across 35 states, we've listened to hundreds of student entries to select the very best for NPR's College Podcast Challenge.
item.dc:creator: Steve Drummond

item.title: When ICE came, Minneapolis created underground health networks. Should other cities?
item.description: The Trump administration's immigration crackdown in Minneapolis forced some families into hiding and catalyzed informal medical networks to deliver critical health care services inside homes.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration's immigration crackdown in Minneapolis forced some families into hiding and catalyzed informal medical networks to deliver critical health care services inside homes.

(Image credit: Kate Wells)
item.dc:creator: Kate Wells

item.title: A run for their money: Young candidates rival older incumbents in midterm fundraising
item.description: As a growing crop of young candidates challenge longtime Democratic incumbents, some are not just breaking through in the money race, but outraising their opponents altogether.
item.content:encoded:
As a growing crop of young candidates challenge longtime Democratic incumbents, some are not just breaking through in the money race, but outraising their opponents altogether.

(Image credit: Jan Sonnenmair)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Announcing the 2025 NPR College Podcast Challenge Honorable Mentions
item.description: Here are some of the best entries in NPR's 2025 College Podcast Challenge.
item.content:encoded: Here are some of the best entries in NPR's 2025 College Podcast Challenge.
item.dc:creator: Student Podcast Challenge

item.title: China sets a lower economic growth target of 4.5% to 5% for 2026 as challenges loom
item.description: China has signaled continuity rather than change for its economy, setting a slightly lower target for growth this year in the midst of a property slump and other headwinds at home and growing uncertainty abroad.
item.content:encoded:
China has signaled continuity rather than change for its economy, setting a slightly lower target for growth this year in the midst of a property slump and other headwinds at home and growing uncertainty abroad.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Carney says he backs strikes on Iran 'with some regret' as world order frays
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says he supports the strikes on Iran "with some regret" as they represent an extreme example of a rupturing world order.
item.content:encoded:
Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says he supports the strikes on Iran "with some regret" as they represent an extreme example of a rupturing world order.

(Image credit: Adrian Wyld/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
